title: Invoke-Expression (iex) with dynamic string input enables code injection
description: Invoke-Expression executes arbitrary strings as PowerShell code. When the string contains any variable interpolation from external sources (user input, file content, API responses), it becomes a code injection vector. Must use the call operator (&) or Invoke-Command with argument splatting instead.
detection_method: Regex scan for Invoke-Expression or iex calls with variable/subexpression arguments
preventionRule: Never use Invoke-Expression with dynamic content. Use & (call operator) for command invocation, or [scriptblock]::Create() with controlled input for dynamic code.
remedy: Replace Invoke-Expression with & operator: instead of iex "Get-Process $name" use & 'Get-Process' -Name $name. For dynamic commands, use & (Get-Command $cmdName) with validated input.
